explanation: The sentence describes a start-up requirement. Whenever the system is powered on ('turned on'), it must load default parameter values from a configuration file. This can be formalized as: whenever the system starts (powered on, represented by '!off' since 'off' means the system is de-energized), globally, it must eventually load the default parameters ('defaultParamsLoaded'). Since powering on is a persistent state (from the moment the system is on until it is off again), we use 'G(!off -> F defaultParamsLoaded)'. If we prefer to make it explicit that when the system becomes 'on' (i.e., after not being 'off'), at some future point the 'defaultParamsLoaded' should hold, the formula is the same.

explanation_dict.natural_language: whenever system is turned on, eventually default values loaded

explanation: The statement says that when the machine is turned on, default parameter values must be loaded from a configuration file. In temporal logic, this is captured by saying: whenever the system starts initialization (initStart), it is guaranteed that eventually (F) the default parameters get loaded (defaultParamsLoaded). In addition, we can refine this by using a global operator: Globally, whenever initialization starts, default parameters will eventually be loaded. We ignore the extra information about the parameters being 'listed in Section 3.1.1' as it's not part of the formal property. Thus, the formula is G(initStart -> F defaultParamsLoaded).

explanation_dict.natural_language: default values...shall be loaded from a configuration file
when the machine is turned on, default values shall be loaded
always, if the machine is turned on, default values will eventually be loaded

explanation: The requirement states that a set of default parameter values must be loaded from a configuration file whenever the machine is turned on (i.e., powered on). Thus, every time we detect the machine is powered on, it must eventually be the case that the default parameters are loaded. This can be represented as: globally (if StartUpMode then eventually defaultParamsLoaded). Here, 'when it is turned on' is mapped to 'StartUpMode', and 'default values loaded' is mapped to 'defaultParamsLoaded', and 'shall be provided and loaded' means that after 'turn on', loading should happen eventually.

explanation_dict.natural_language: a set of default values for all parameters shall be provided and loaded from a configuration file
whenever machine is turned on, eventually default values are loaded

explanation: The requirement states that whenever the machine is turned on, a set of default values for all parameters shall be provided and loaded from a configuration file. We can map the machine being turned on to the negation of 'off' (i.e., the machine is on when 'off' is false). The loading of default parameters is represented by the atomic proposition 'defaultParamsLoaded'. Thus, the specification can be expressed as: Whenever the machine transitions to being on (i.e., it is not off), eventually default parameters are loaded. To express 'on' as a state, we can use '!off'. Thus, globally, if the machine is on, then eventually (in the future) defaultParamsLoaded must be true: G((!off) -> F(defaultParamsLoaded)).

explanation_dict.natural_language: Whenever the machine is on, eventually default parameters are loaded
Globally, enforce the above whenever the machine is on

explanation: The requirement says that (1) when the machine is turned on, (2) default parameter values must be provided and loaded from a configuration file. We are interested in system-level behavior associated with start-up. The atomic proposition relevant here is 'defaultParamsLoaded', indicating that the default parameters were loaded, and possibly 'StartUpMode', indicating that the start-up mode is enabled. The phrase 'when it is turned on' refers to the start of system operation, but LTL typically expresses ongoing behaviors, so we can state that globally, if the system enters StartUpMode, eventually, default parameters will be loaded. This is written as G(StartUpMode -> F defaultParamsLoaded).

explanation_dict.natural_language: when in Start-up Mode, eventually defaultParamsLoaded

explanation: The requirement is: when the machine is turned on (leaves off state), a set of default parameter values must be loaded from a configuration file. In terms of LTL, this means: once the machine powers on, eventually (F) the default params will be loaded. We suppose the atomic propositions 'off' (machine is off), and 'defaultParamsLoaded' (defaults loaded) are present. We express 'turned on' as leaving the 'off' state (¬off now or X¬off for next) and 'loading the defaults' as defaultParamsLoaded.  More precisely, globally, when the system transitions from 'off' to 'on' (i.e., off is true now and not true in the next state), then, from that point, it is required that eventually the default parameters will be loaded. That is,G((off & X(!off)) -> F(defaultParamsLoaded))

explanation_dict.natural_language: default parameters shall be provided and loaded
if machine is turned on then eventually default parameters are loaded

explanation: The sentence describes the system's requirement for start-up mode: When the machine is turned on (i.e., transitioning to StartUpMode), the default parameters must be loaded from the configuration file. In LTL, this can be expressed as: whenever (globally) we enter StartUpMode, eventually the default parameters will be loaded (defaultParamsLoaded). Thus, we use a global implication: G(StartUpMode -> F defaultParamsLoaded).

explanation_dict.natural_language: when it is turned on (start-up mode)
default values for all parameters shall be provided and loaded from configuration file
if machine enters start-up mode, then eventually default parameters are loaded

explanation: The requirement says that a set of default parameter values must be provided and loaded from a configuration file when the machine is turned on. This means: (1) When the machine is turned on, at some point the loading of default parameters must occur. We represent 'machine is turned on' as not 'off' (since there's no atomic proposition directly for turned on) and 'default parameters loaded' as defaultParamsLoaded. The phrase means that always (globally), if the system is on, eventually the default parameters will be loaded. In LTL, this is expressed as G((!off) -> F defaultParamsLoaded).

explanation_dict.natural_language: if machine is turned on, eventually default parameters shall be loaded

explanation: The requirement states that whenever the machine is turned on, default values for all parameters are provided and loaded from a configuration file. Mapping this to LTL, we need to express that globally, if the system is turned on, then eventually the default parameters are loaded. Let 'on' represent the machine being turned on (which we can encode as '!off'), and 'defaultParamsLoaded' represent default parameters being loaded. The formula expresses that globally (G), whenever the machine is not off, then eventually (F) defaultParamsLoaded holds. Optional: If you want a more strict interpretation (parameters must always be loaded exactly at power-on), you could encode 'X(defaultParamsLoaded)' right after 'on'. But with the available atomic propositions, the direct mapping is: G((!off) -> F defaultParamsLoaded).

explanation_dict.natural_language: default values ... loaded from a configuration file
whenever the machine is turned on, eventually default values are loaded

explanation:
The requirement says that when the machine is turned on (i.e., leaves the off state), a predefined set of parameter values should be loaded from a configuration file. We will encode this by saying that globally: whenever the machine is no longer in 'off' (has left the off state), then eventually the default parameters are loaded ('defaultParamsLoaded') must hold true. This uses the implication operator (->), the 'off' atomic proposition (meaning system is de-energized), and the 'defaultParamsLoaded' atomic proposition. The 'eventually' is represented by the F operator, and 'globally' by G.

So the structure is: G((!off) -> F(defaultParamsLoaded))
- 'Globally' (G): this condition should always be enforced.
- 'If the machine is not off' (!off): this means the system is on (turned on).
- 'then eventually...' (-> F(defaultParamsLoaded)): upon turning on, at some future point, the default parameters are loaded.

Alternatively, if you want it to occur just at the very moment the machine is turned on (i.e., on the transition from 'off' to '!off'), you could use the X (next) operator, but the classical form is as above: G((!off) -> F(defaultParamsLoaded)).

explanation_dict.natural_language: If machine is turned on then eventually default parameters are loaded
